data.text:
Now I understand the project. The user wants me to implement "STP protocol" (Segment-Target-Position) — which is Kotler's STP marketing framework (Segmentation, Targeting, Positioning). The user said "dominate it" — make it a prominent/dominant feature in the marketing hub.

Looking at the codebase:
- `/strategy/positioning` page exists (Positioning, part of STP)
- `/segments` page exists (Segmentation, part of STP)
- `/personas` page exists (likely related to targeting)
- There's a strategy home page at `/strategy` with workbenches
- The positioning workbench sub is "Kotler's STP + Positioning"

The user wants STP protocol (Segment-Target-Position) to be a dominant feature. I should create a unified STP workflow/page that ties segmentation, targeting, and positioning together.

Let me look at the existing positioning page, personas page, and any existing targeting infrastructure to understand what's there.

data.text:
Now I understand the project. The marketing-hub project has:
- `/strategy/positioning` - the "P" (Position) part of STP — Kotler's positioning workbench
- `/personas` - audience personas (which represents the "T" — Target)
- `/segments` - audience segments (which represents the "S" — Segment)

STP = Segmentation, Targeting, Positioning. Currently these three live in three separate places:
- Segment → `/segments` (segments page)
- Target → `/personas` (personas page)
- Position → `/strategy/positioning` (positioning workbench)

The strategy page at `/strategy` mentions "Kotler's STP + Positioning" as the sub of the Positioning workbench but the three are disconnected.

"stp protocol in the marketing hub dominate it" — The user wants me to implement the STP (Segment, Target, Position) protocol as a dominant/unifying framework in the marketing-hub. That means creating a unified STP experience that ties together segmentation, targeting, and positioning.

Let me think about what "dominate it" means. Likely they want STP to become the dominant/unifying strategy framework — a single hub that brings together the three pieces with a clear protocol/workflow that guides through segment → target → position.

Let me check the frameworks file and the sidebar to understand the existing structure and the API routes available.

Now I understand the layout. The user wants me to "dominate" STP (Segment, Target, Position) protocol in the marketing hub project. The project already has separate pages for:
- `/segments` — audience segments (rule-based filtering)
- `/personas` — audience personas (ICP)
- `/strategy/positioning` — positioning workbench (Kotler STP + Positioning)

The user wants me to make STP "dominant" — meaning unify these into a cohesive STP protocol/workbench that's the central strategy workflow. Let me think about what "dominate it" means here. It's vague, but I think they want me to make STP the dominant/central organizing framework in the marketing hub.

Let me clarify the intent rather than guessing. Actually, let me re-read: "stp protocol in the marketing hub dominate it". This is ambiguous. It could mean:
1. Make the STP protocol dominant/central in the marketing hub (elevate it)
2. Implement the STP protocol as the dominant framework

Given the project already has the pieces scattered (segments, personas, positioning), the most valuable interpretation is: create a unified STP workbench that ties Segment → Target → Position together as the dominant strategy flow, and elevate it in the navigation.

But this is a significant amount of work and the request is genuinely ambiguous. Let me ask a clarifying question to nail down what "dominate it" means.

Actually, given the personality guidance to not ask for more info than necessary and accomplish tasks, but this is genuinely ambiguous on a large task. Let me ask one focused question.

I've explored the marketing-hub project. STP (Segment → Target → Position) already exists but is split across three separate, disconnected pages:

- `/segments` — rule-based audience segmentation
- `/personas` — ICP/persona cards (the "Target" step)
- `/strategy/positioning` — Kotler positioning workbench (the "Position" step)

The Strategy home (`/strategy`) lists Positioning as one of four workbenches, but there's no unified STP flow linking the three steps together, and Segment/Target live outside `/strategy`.

Before I build, I need to pin down what "dominate it" means to you:

1: import { NextRequest } from "next/server";
2: import { json, err, readJson, listRows, insertRow } from "@/lib/api-helpers";
3: import { newId, now } from "@/lib/db";
4: 
5: export const dynamic = "force-dynamic";
6: 
7: export async function GET() {
8:   return json(listRows("segments"));
9: }
10: 
11: export async function POST(req: NextRequest) {
12:   const body = await readJson<Record<string, unknown>>(req);
13:   if (!body || !body.name) return err("name required");
14:   const id = newId("seg_");
15:   const t = now();
16:   const row = insertRow("segments", {
17:     id,
18:     name: body.name,
19:     description: body.description || "",
20:     rules: body.rules || [],
21:     contact_count: body.contact_count || 0,
22:     color: body.color || "#888888",
23:     created_at: t,
24:     updated_at: t,
25:   });
26:   return json(row, 201);
27: }

(End of file - total 27 lines)

  | "7c"; // Clear → Concise → Compelling → Credible → Current → Consistent → Conversion-led

  category:

    | "Customer"

};

 export const POSITIONING_TEMPLATE =
   "For [TARGET] who [NEED/OPPORTUNITY], [BRAND] is the [CATEGORY/FR-OF-REFERENCE] that [PROMISE/POINTS-OF-DIFFERENCE] because [REASONS-TO-BELIEVE].";
 
+/**
+ * The big strategy catalog — every framework a marketer reaches for, grouped
+ * by the decision it helps make. Used by the STP protocol page so strategy
+ * dominates from one place.
+ *
+ * Categories mirror the marketing decision stack:
+ *   positioning · competitive · growth · brand · customer · channel · analysis
+ */

+  id: string;
+  label: string;
+  author: string;
+  category:
+    | "Positioning"
+    | "Competitive"
+    | "Growth"
+    | "Brand"
+    | "Customer"
+    | "Channel"
+    | "Analysis";
+  oneLine: string;
+  steps: string[];
+  when: string;
+  pairsWith?: string[];
+};

input.content:
# Plan: Strategy Frameworks with Execution Tools

## Current state
- 42 strategy frameworks in `src/lib/frameworks.ts` (`STRATEGY_FRAMEWORKS`)
- Rendered as expandable cards on `/strategy/stp` (Strategy Library panel)
- No dedicated `/strategy/frameworks` page
- No execution tools — frameworks are reference-only

## Goal
Add execution tools (scorecards, templates, calculators, decision trees) to strategy frameworks so users can act on them, not just read them. Deliver a standalone `/strategy/frameworks` page.

## Decisions

| Decision | Choice |
|---|---|
| Tool types | `scorecard`, `template`, `calculator`, `decision_tree` |
| Execution UI | Expandable panel inline on the frameworks page (no new route per tool) |
| Compute location | Client-side in tool components (follows `library.ts` Formula pattern) |
| Coverage target | 20 of 42 frameworks get tools; rest show "Reference only" |
| API | None — compute client-side; no server state needed |

## Data model changes

**File:** `src/lib/frameworks.ts`

Extend `StrategyFramework`:
```ts
type StrategyTool = {
  type: "scorecard" | "template" | "calculator" | "decision_tree";
  title: string;
  inputs: Array<{
    id: string;
    label: string;
    type: "select" | "number" | "slider" | "text" | "radio";
    options?: string[];
    min?: number;
    max?: number;
    default?: number | string;
  }>;
  compute?: (inputs: Record<string, any>) => { score?: number; verdict?: string; output?: string };
  scoring?: Array<{ max: number; label: string; color: string }>;
};

type StrategyFramework = {
  ...
  tool?: StrategyTool;
};
```

Add `tool` field to 20 existing frameworks (STP, Positioning Statement, Porter Generic, Blue Ocean, SWOT, Ansoff, BCG, AARRR, 7Ps, 4Ps, Brand Pyramid, Awareness Levels, RFM, NPS, Customer Journey, PESTEL, OKR, Balanced Scorecard, Jobs-to-be-Done, Category Creation).

## New files

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `src/app/strategy/frameworks/page.tsx` | Standalone browsable strategy catalog with execution |
| `src/components/strategy/ScorecardTool.tsx` | Slider/radio inputs + score gauge + verdict |
| `src/components/strategy/TemplateTool.tsx` | Fill-in-the-blank fields + generated output |
| `src/components/strategy/CalculatorTool.tsx` | Number inputs + computed result display |
| `src/components/strategy/DecisionTreeTool.tsx` | Step-by-step yes/no flow with branching |

## Modified files

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/lib/frameworks.ts` | Add `StrategyTool` type, add `tool` to 20 frameworks |
| `src/app/strategy/stp/page.tsx` | Link Strategy Library cards to `/strategy/frameworks` instead of inline expansion |
| `src/components/Sidebar.tsx` | Add `Frameworks` entry under Strategy group |
| `src/app/strategy/page.tsx` | Add Frameworks workbench card (or link to STP if keeping it minimal) |

## Execution tool patterns

### Scorecard (e.g., Positioning Scorecard)
- 5–8 criteria sliders (0–10)
- Compute weighted average → score out of 100
- Show verdict: "Strong / Needs work / Weak" with color
- Example: Positioning Scorecard → clarity, differentiation, proof, relevance, simplicity

### Template (e.g., Positioning Statement Template)
- 4–6 text inputs (target, frame of reference, difference, proof, brand)
- Live preview of the filled template
- Copy-to-clipboard button
- Example: fills the `POSITIONING_TEMPLATE` with user input

### Calculator (e.g., BCG Matrix Calculator)
- 2 number inputs per quadrant (market growth %, relative market share)
- Compute quadrant placement for each of 4 products
- Show visual 2×2 matrix with plotted products
- Example: BCG → star/cash-cow/dog/question-mark classification

### Decision tree (e.g., Market Entry Decision Tree)
- 6–8 yes/no questions with branching
- Each answer advances to next question
- Final verdict with recommended strategy
- Example: "Should we enter this market?" → questions on size, competition, differentiation, etc.

## UI flow

1. User visits `/strategy/frameworks`
2. Sees searchable/filterable grid of all 42+ frameworks
3. Each card shows title, author, category, one-liner
4. Frameworks with tools show an "Execute" badge/button
5. Clicking "Execute" expands the tool inline below the card (or opens a Modal for calculators/decision trees)
6. User fills inputs → sees live result
7. Scorecards show gauge; templates show preview; calculators show result; decision trees show path

## Validation

- `tsc --noEmit` passes for all changed files
- Verify each tool type renders correctly in dev (`npm run dev`)
- Check that frameworks without tools still render as read-only cards
- Verify sidebar navigation to `/strategy/frameworks` works
- Verify STP Strategy Library links redirect correctly

## Out of scope

- Persisting tool results to DB (no `/api/strategy-tools` endpoint needed)
- AI-generated strategy recommendations (keep tools deterministic)
- Mobile-specific tool layouts (use existing responsive patterns)
- Framework CRUD (frameworks are static catalog data)

+
+## Execution tool patterns
+
+### Scorecard (e.g., Positioning Scorecard)
+- 5–8 criteria sliders (0–10)
+- Compute weighted average → score out of 100
+- Show verdict: "Strong / Needs work / Weak" with color
+- Example: Positioning Scorecard → clarity, differentiation, proof, relevance, simplicity
+
+### Template (e.g., Positioning Statement Template)
+- 4–6 text inputs (target, frame of reference, difference, proof, brand)
+- Live preview of the filled template

+
+### Calculator (e.g., BCG Matrix Calculator)

+

+# Plan: Strategy Frameworks with Execution Tools
+
+## Current state
+- 42 strategy frameworks in `src/lib/frameworks.ts` (`STRATEGY_FRAMEWORKS`)
+- Rendered as expandable cards on `/strategy/stp` (Strategy Library panel)
+- No dedicated `/strategy/frameworks` page
+- No execution tools — frameworks are reference-only
+
+## Goal
+Add execution tools (scorecards, templates, calculators, decision trees) to strategy frameworks so users can act on them, not just read them. Deliver a standalone `/strategy/frameworks` page.
+
+## Decisions
+
+| Decision | Choice |
+|---|---|
+| Tool types | `scorecard`, `template`, `calculator`, `decision_tree` |
+| Execution UI | Expandable panel inline on the frameworks page (no new route per tool) |
+| Compute location | Client-side in tool components (follows `library.ts` Formula pattern) |
+| Coverage target | 20 of 42 frameworks get tools; rest show "Reference only" |
+| API | None — compute client-side; no server state needed |
+
+## Data model changes
+
+**File:** `src/lib/frameworks.ts`
+
+Extend `StrategyFramework`:
+```ts
+type StrategyTool = {
+  type: "scorecard" | "template" | "calculator" | "decision_tree";
+  title: string;
+  inputs: Array<{
+    id: string;
+    label: string;
+    type: "select" | "number" | "slider" | "text" | "radio";
+    options?: string[];
+    min?: number;
+    max?: number;
+    default?: number | string;
+  }>;
+  compute?: (inputs: Record<string, any>) => { score?: number; verdict?: string; output?: string };
+  scoring?: Array<{ max: number; label: string; color: string }>;
+};
+
+type StrategyFramework = {
+  ...
+  tool?: StrategyTool;
+};
+```
+
+Add `tool` field to 20 existing frameworks (STP, Positioning Statement, Porter Generic, Blue Ocean, SWOT, Ansoff, BCG, AARRR, 7Ps, 4Ps, Brand Pyramid, Awareness Levels, RFM, NPS, Customer Journey, PESTEL, OKR, Balanced Scorecard, Jobs-to-be-Done, Category Creation).
+
+## New files
+
+| File | Purpose |
+|---|---|
+| `src/app/strategy/frameworks/page.tsx` | Standalone browsable strategy catalog with execution |
+| `src/components/strategy/ScorecardTool.tsx` | Slider/radio inputs + score gauge + verdict |
+| `src/components/strategy/TemplateTool.tsx` | Fill-in-the-blank fields + generated output |
+| `src/components/strategy/CalculatorTool.tsx` | Number inputs + computed result display |
+| `src/components/strategy/DecisionTreeTool.tsx` | Step-by-step yes/no flow with branching |
+
+## Modified files
+
+| File | Change |
+|---|---|
+| `src/lib/frameworks.ts` | Add `StrategyTool` type, add `tool` to 20 frameworks |
+| `src/app/strategy/stp/page.tsx` | Link Strategy Library cards to `/strategy/frameworks` instead of inline expansion |
+| `src/components/Sidebar.tsx` | Add `Frameworks` entry under Strategy group |
+| `src/app/strategy/page.tsx` | Add Frameworks workbench card (or link to STP if keeping it minimal) |
+
+## Execution tool patterns
+
+### Scorecard (e.g., Positioning Scorecard)
+- 5–8 criteria sliders (0–10)
+- Compute weighted average → score out of 100
+- Show verdict: "Strong / Needs work / Weak" with color
+- Example: Positioning Scorecard → clarity, differentiation, proof, relevance, simplicity
+
+### Template (e.g., Positioning Statement Template)
+- 4–6 text inputs (target, frame of reference, difference, proof, brand)
+- Live preview of the filled template
+- Copy-to-clipboard button
+- Example: fills the `POSITIONING_TEMPLATE` with user input
+
+### Calculator (e.g., BCG Matrix Calculator)
+- 2 number inputs per quadrant (market growth %, relative market share)
+- Compute quadrant placement for each of 4 products
+- Show visual 2×2 matrix with plotted products
+- Example: BCG → star/cash-cow/dog/question-mark classification
+
+### Decision tree (e.g., Market Entry Decision Tree)
+- 6–8 yes/no questions with branching
+- Each answer advances to next question
+- Final verdict with recommended strategy
+- Example: "Should we enter this market?" → questions on size, competition, differentiation, etc.
+
+## UI flow
+
+1. User visits `/strategy/frameworks`
+2. Sees searchable/filterable grid of all 42+ frameworks
+3. Each card shows title, author, category, one-liner
+4. Frameworks with tools show an "Execute" badge/button
+5. Clicking "Execute" expands the tool inline below the card (or opens a Modal for calculators/decision trees)
+6. User fills inputs → sees live result
+7. Scorecards show gauge; templates show preview; calculators show result; decision trees show path

   when: "Growth funnel diagnostics.",
+  tool: {
+    type: "scorecard",
+    title: "AARRR Funnel Health Scorecard",
+    description: "Rate each funnel stage. The weakest stage is your bottleneck — fix it first.",
+    inputs: [
+      { id: "acquisition", label: "Acquisition — are you reaching enough of the right people?", type: "slider", min: 0, max: 10, default: 5 },
+      { id: "activation", label: "Activation — do visitors reach the aha-moment?", type: "slider", min: 0, max: 10, default: 5 },
+      { id: "retention", label: "Retention — do they come back?", type: "slider", min: 0, max: 10, default: 5 },
+      { id: "referral", label: "Referral — do they bring others?", type: "slider", min: 0, max: 10, default: 5 },
+      { id: "revenue", label: "Revenue — do they pay and grow?", type: "slider", min: 0, max: 10, default: 5 },
+    ],
+    scoring: [
+      { max: 40, label: "Broken funnel — rebuild from the weakest stage", color: "#ef4444" },
+      { max: 60, label: "Leaky — plug the biggest gap", color: "#f59e0b" },
+      { max: 80, label: "Healthy — optimize incrementally", color: "#0ea5e9" },
+      { max: 100, label: "Excellent — scale it", color: "#16a34a" },
+    ],
+    compute: (i) => {
+      const keys = ["acquisition", "activation", "retention", "referral", "revenue"];
+      const vals = keys.map((k) => Number(i[k] || 0));
+      const sum = vals.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0);
